channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former 'American Idol' contestant pleads guilty in deadly DUI crash
item.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Kennedy has pleaded guilty to driving under the influence during a deadly 2022 crash in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Robert Cox, Fox 5

item.title: Carcass of endangered fin whale washes up near Alaska's largest city
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An endangered fin whale that washed up near a coastal trail in Alaska's largest city has attracted curious onlookers while biologists seek answers as to what caused the animal's death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare coin found in piece of furniture sells for $2.52M at auction
item.description: A rare 17th Century coin sold at auction for a record-breaking $2.52 million eight years after it was found in an old cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apartment buildings wrap up $1B in HUD funds for lower utility bills and climate upgrades
item.description: Renters in affordable housing felt forgotten and left out of the nation's transition to clean energy. They lived in older buildings that had been repaired over the years but had leaky windows and old appliances that consumed a lot of energy. They didn't have solar power.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump assassination attempt task force subpoenas 2 ATF employees, alleging stonewalling
item.description: Congressional lawmakers have accused the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) of stonewalling their requests for documents on the assassination attempts against Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reuters hires former Post and AP executive editor Sally Buzbee to lead U.S., Canada news operation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sally Buzbee, the former executive editor of The Washington Post and The Associated Press, is joining Reuters as the news agency's top editor for the United States and Canada, the company said Tuesday. As news editor for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecution is expected to rest in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Georgia nursing student Laken Riley texted and called her mom as she headed out for a run to see whether she had time to chat — but then didn't respond to her mother's calls or increasingly frantic text messages. Riley called her mother at 9:03 a.m. on Feb.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The White House's Christmas tree is a symbol of resilience for hurricane-hit North Carolina farms
item.description: NEWLAND, N.C. (AP) — The Cartner family had known since last year that one of their farm’s Christmas trees would be headed to the White House this winter.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries wins reelection as House Democratic leader
item.description: House Leader Hakeem Jeffries won reelection Tuesday as the Democratic leader, receiving support from his colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haitian migrants reportedly flee Springfield, Ohio
item.description: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, have begun leaving the town in the wake of President-elect Trump’s sweeping election win, after Trump vowed mass deportation of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: California theme park riders suspended in air for over 2 hours
item.description: Thrill-seekers at Southern California amusement park Knott's Berry Farm were unexpectedly stuck on one of the theme park's rides for over two hours Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Top Democrat on House Ethics panel says Gaetz report should be released
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (D-Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, said she thinks the panel’s report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) should be released.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Sister of ex-Marine charged in subway chokehold death testifies to his 'calm' spirit and patriotism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The older sister of Daniel Penny, the former U.S. Marine charged with fatally choking a homeless man aboard a Manhattan subway, told jurors Monday that her brother was a “calm, soft-spirited person” with a reputation for honesty and integrity.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal appeals court won't block upcoming nitrogen gas execution in Alabama
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — A federal appeals court said Monday it will let the nation’s third execution with nitrogen gas go forward this week in Alabama, rejecting arguments that the new method causes unconstitutional levels of pain. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father of Oxford shooting victim calls on community for help
item.description: Ethan Crumbley killed four students and injured several others in October of 2021 using a gun gifted by his father.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: 2 killed, 10 wounded in shootings near New Orleans parade route
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Two people were killed and 10 others were wounded in two separate shootings along a New Orleans parade route and celebration attended by thousands on Sunday, authorities said. There were no immediate arrests. Officers responding to reports of gunfire shortly after 3:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 hurt when plane crashes near Southern California dragstrip hosting racing event
item.description: At least two parked vehicles on the ground were struck when the small plane crashed around near the dragstrip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: Just days after being tapped to co-lead the Department of Government Efficiency, Elon Musk is searching for "high-IQ" individuals to join the taskforce.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: 8-year-old who raised money for mother's headstone now giving back
item.description: Emouree Johnson, 8, ran a lemonade stand earlier this year to raise money for a headstone for her mother. Now, she's raising funds to help other kids.
item.dc:creator: Josiah Elmore, KRON4

item.title: Bullet strikes Southwest Airlines flight in Dallas
item.description: The Texas incident comes just days after JetBlue Airways, Spirit Airlines and American Airlines halted flights to Haiti after two aircraft were hit by bullets amid the gang violence escalating in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: US health officials report 1st case of new form of mpox in a traveler
item.description: Health officials said Saturday they have confirmed the first U.S. case of a new form of mpox that was first seen in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's is investing $100 million to bring customers back after E. coli outbreak
item.description: McDonald's is investing $100 million to bring customers back to stores after an outbreak of E. coli food poisoning tied to onions on the fast-food giant's Quarter Pounder hamburgers. The investments include $65 million that will go directly to the hardest-hit franchises, the company said. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No. 13 Purdue rediscovers strong post presence in 87-78 win over No. 2 Alabama
item.description: Boilermakers forward Trey Kaufman-Renn made 12 of 23 shots, scored a career-high 26 points and grabbed eight rebounds as No. 13 Purdue handed No. 2 Alabama an 87-78 defeat that was eerily reminiscent of last season's 92-86 loss to the Boilermakers in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmaker says Oklahoma state superintendent's 'Prayer to the Nation' video is indoctrination
item.description: A recorded prayer was emailed in an unlisted YouTube link to Oklahoma school superintendents yesterday, telling them to have the video shared in classrooms and with parents.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why you should throw away your black plastic cooking utensils
item.description: The tools you cook with may be introducing an unwanted ingredient into your family dinner: flame retardant.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Giuliani's lawyers seek to withdraw from legal fight over $148M defamation judgment against him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two lawyers say they can no longer represent Rudy Giuliani in a legal fight over property he's been ordered to give up to satisfy part of a $148 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Satire publication The Onion buys Alex Jones' Infowars at auction with Sandy Hook families' backing
item.description: The satirical news publication The Onion was named the winning bidder for Alex Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction Thursday, backed by families of Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting victims whom Jones owes more than $1 billion in defamation judgments for calling the massacre a hoax.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prominent conservative lawyer Ted Olson, who argued Bush recount and same-sex marriage cases, dies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former U.S. Solicitor General Ted Olson, who served two Republican presidents as one of the country’s best known conservative lawyers and successfully argued on behalf of same-sex marriage, died Wednesday. He was 84.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
